Home / Services / Drip Irrigation Systems

Drip Irrigation Systems

Targeted watering for drought-tolerant landscapes and vegetable gardens, minimizing evaporation loss.

Perfect for Sacramento's Mediterranean climate, our drip systems deliver water directly to plant roots with 90% efficiency. We design different zones for native plants versus edible gardens - accounting for the Valley Oak Charter School's demonstration garden needs versus backyard tomatoes in Curtis Park.

Using pressure-compensating emitters prevents uneven watering during peak summer demand when municipal pressure fluctuates. For sloped properties common in Fair Oaks, we install anti-drain valves to prevent dry spots.

All tubing is UV-resistant to withstand our intense sunlight, with buried mainlines to protect against gopher damage prevalent in Carmichael soils. Winterization includes blowing out lines to prevent freezing damage during rare cold snaps.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Mapping plant groupings and water requirements
  2. 02 Step 2: Calculating optimal emitter placement and flow rates
  3. 03 Step 3: Installing color-coded tubing for easy maintenance
  4. 04 Step 4: Pressure testing and mulch integration

Benefits

  • Ideal for California native plants
  • Reduces weed growth by avoiding surface watering
  • Prevents fungal diseases common in humid microclimates
  • Works with rainwater harvesting systems

Frequently Asked Questions

Can drip irrigation work with my existing sprinklers?

We often create hybrid systems, keeping spray heads for lawns while converting planting beds to drip. This approach works well for Tudor-style homes in College Greens with mixed landscaping.

How often should the system be flushed?

We recommend annual flushing before summer peak season, included in our maintenance plans. Homes near the American River with silt-heavy water may need semi-annual service.

Get a Free Estimate
